title: Walsh administration pushes to start biggest Syracuse housing development in decades
summary: Mayor Walsh's administration is advancing a $134M mixed-income housing development at the former Syracuse Developmental Center site, planning to break ground in 2026. The project would deliver 260 affordable apartments, 27 townhomes, and future phases including market-rate and senior housing—potentially Syracuse's largest housing development in 50+ years. The Common Council must approve financing mechanisms and infrastructure bonding today, though some council members express concerns about mixed-income sustainability.

political_signal.description: Walsh administration prioritizing major housing development as transformational infrastructure project; Council Chair Hogan applying scrutiny to financing structure and mixed-income sustainability; state funding commitment signals intergovernmental priority alignment.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts in mechanical extraction list appear to include extraneous data unrelated to article content; primary figures validated: $134M (project cost), $11M (site work loan), $18.8M (infrastructure bonding), $29M (demolition grant), $72,500 (median income threshold).
Article focuses on Phase 1 financing approval timing; future phases remain contingent on developer performance and city land control.
Council member scrutiny suggests potential legislative process delay beyond stated voting timeline.
Missing author byline attribution but article sourced to syracuse.com with reporter Tim Knauss.
